title: Converting and Combining PDFs
description: Learn how to merge multiple PDFs into one and split large PDFs for easier sharing and management.
objectives: Merge multiple PDFs into one document
Split a large PDF into smaller sections
Understand file size limits for email
Compress PDFs to reduce file size
content:
Merging PDFs

In the workplace, you often need to combine multiple documents into one file. For example, a grant application might require a cover letter, budget, and references. Sending three separate files is messy. Sending one combined PDF is professional and easier for the recipient to manage.

How to Merge

Adobe Acrobat Reader (free version) and many online tools let you merge PDFs by dragging files into a sequence. Always check the final combined PDF to make sure pages are in the correct order. A common mistake is merging files in alphabetical order instead of logical order.

Splitting Large PDFs

Email systems often limit attachments to 10MB or 25MB. If your PDF is too large, split it into smaller sections. For example, a 50-page annual report could be split into Executive Summary, Financials, and Appendices. This also makes the document easier for readers to navigate.

Reordering Pages

If you merge files in the wrong order, use the thumbnail panel in your PDF viewer to drag pages into the correct sequence. Save the reordered document with a clear name like 2024-Report_CORRECTED.pdf to avoid confusion.

Compressing PDFs

PDF compression reduces file size without significantly affecting quality. This is useful when you need to email a large PDF or upload it to a website with file size limits. Many PDF tools and online services offer compression options.
exercise: Create three one-page PDFs from Word documents. Name them Part1, Part2, and Part3. Combine them into one PDF named CombinedDocument.pdf. Check that the pages are in the correct order. Then compress the file using any free PDF compression tool and note the before and after file sizes.

quiz.question: Why is it better to send one combined PDF instead of three separate files?
options: It is more professional and easier for the recipient to manage
quiz.explanation: A single PDF is easier to open, print, and file than multiple separate attachments.

quiz.question: What should you do if a PDF is too large to email?
options: Split it into smaller sections or compress it
quiz.explanation: Splitting or compressing the PDF keeps the content accessible while respecting email size limits.

quiz.question: How do you fix pages that are in the wrong order after merging?
options: Use the thumbnail panel to drag pages into the correct order
Delete the file and start over
quiz.explanation: Most PDF viewers allow you to drag and drop page thumbnails to reorder pages before saving.

quiz.question: Why is it important to check the order of pages after merging PDFs?
options: To ensure the document flows logically for the reader
quiz.explanation: Checking the page order ensures the combined document makes sense and follows the correct logical sequence.
